game_name: Chester Cheetah: Too Cool to Fool

danger_signals.visual_form: Antagonistic characters positioned in level paths
danger_signals.meaning: Obstacles to overcome; threat to progress
Environmental dangers requiring avoidance or timing

safety_signals.meaning: Progression rewards; safe-to-collect items that enable advancement
Agency and escape capability; player has defensive/evasive tools

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cheetos-themed level design (inferred from brand context)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard platform level layout with visible enemies and collectibles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Chester can dash and stun enemies via jumping

environmental_storytelling.example: Cheetos mascot as protagonist positions product as 'cool' and capable; level design likely incorporates snack/food-themed imagery
Chester's 'too cool' personality (from slogan) informs how he interacts with enemies (stunning via jumping suggests confident, carefree approach)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Positioned as obstacles; vulnerable to Chester's jump attack

notes: Game is a simple side-scrolling platformer with limited documented mechanical depth. Wikipedia source provides minimal visual/mechanical detail beyond genre classification and scooter part collectible system. The game's visual communication would follow standard 1992 SNES platformer conventions: bright sprite-based graphics, clear enemy/hazard silhouettes, and color-coded safe/danger zones. Chester's character design (sunglasses, cool posture) from brand context likely carries through to in-game sprite representation. No faction conflict or political systems documented.
